{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_e179af1e23a44b508f494741","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Rendezvous hash-based routing and shard assignment.\n2\t\n3\tuse crate::topology::{Group, NodeId, Topology};\n4\tuse std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n5\tuse twox_hash::XxHash64;\n6\t\n7\t/// Compute a rendezvous score for a shard+node pair.\n8\t///\n9\t/// Higher scores win; used for deterministic shard assignment.\n10\t///\n11\t/// CRITICAL: Uses seed 0 to match Meilisearch Enterprise's hash function.\n12\t/// Any deviation (different seed, different ordering, endianness) forks\n13\t/// routing across any two Miroir instances and silently corrupts writes.\n14\tpub fn score(shard_id: u32, node_id: &str) -> u64 {\n15\t    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n16\t    shard_id.hash(&mut h);\n17\t    node_id.hash(&mut h);\n18\t    h.finish()\n19\t}\n20\t\n21\t/// Assign a shard to `rf` nodes within a single replica group.\n22\t///\n23\t/// `group_nodes` is the subset of nodes belonging to that group.\n24\t///\n25\t/// Nodes are sorted by score descending, with ties broken lexicographically\n26\t/// by node_id to ensure deterministic assignment even when hash scores collide.\n27\tpub fn assign_shard_in_group(shard_id: u32, group_nodes: &[NodeId], rf: usize) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n28\t    let mut scored: Vec<(u64, &NodeId)> = group_nodes\n29\t        .iter()\n30\t        .map(|n| (score(shard_id, n.as_str()), n))\n31\t        .collect();\n32\t    scored.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| {\n33\t        b.0.cmp(&a.0)\n34\t            .then_with(|| a.1.as_str().cmp(b.1.as_str()))\n35\t    });\n36\t    scored\n37\t        .into_iter()\n38\t        .take(rf)\n39\t        .map(|(_, n)| n.clone())\n40\t        .collect()\n41\t}\n42\t\n43\t/// All write targets for a document: the RF nodes in EACH replica group.\n44\tpub fn write_targets(shard_id: u32, topology: &Topology) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n45\t    topology\n46\t        .groups()\n47\t        .flat_map(|group| assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf()))\n48\t        .collect()\n49\t}\n50\t\n51\t/// Select the replica group for a query (round-robin by query counter).\n52\tpub fn query_group(query_seq: u64, replica_groups: u32) -> u32 {\n53\t    (query_seq % replica_groups as u64) as u32\n54\t}\n55\t\n56\t/// The covering set for a search: one node per shard within the chosen group.\n57\t///\n58\t/// Returns a Vec where index i contains the node to query for shard i.\n59\t/// The length is always exactly shard_count, even if multiple shards\n60\t/// map to the same node.\n61\tpub fn covering_set(shard_count: u32, group: &Group, rf: usize, query_seq: u64) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n62\t    (0..shard_count)\n63\t        .map(|shard_id| {\n64\t            let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n65\t            // rotate through replicas for intra-group load balancing\n66\t            replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n67\t        })\n68\t        .collect()\n69\t}\n70\t\n71\t/// Compute the shard ID for a document's primary key.\n72\tpub fn shard_for_key(primary_key: &str, shard_count: u32) -> u32 {\n73\t    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n74\t    primary_key.hash(&mut h);\n75\t    (h.finish() % shard_count as u64) as u32\n76\t}\n77\t\n78\t#[cfg(test)]\n79\tmod tests {\n80\t    use super::*;\n81\t    use crate::topology::{Node, NodeId};\n82\t\n83\t    // Test 1: Rendezvous assignment is deterministic given fixed node list\n84\t    #[test]\n85\t    fn test_rendezvous_determinism() {\n86\t        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n87\t            .into_iter()\n88\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n89\t            .collect();\n90\t        let shard_id = 42;\n91\t\n92\t        let assignment1 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, 1);\n93\t        let assignment2 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, 1);\n94\t\n95\t        assert_eq!(assignment1, assignment2);\n96\t    }\n97\t\n98\t    // Test 2: Score is stable across calls\n99\t    #[test]\n100\t    fn test_score_stability() {\n101\t        let score1 = score(123, \"node1\");\n102\t        let score2 = score(123, \"node1\");\n103\t        assert_eq!(score1, score2);\n104\t    }\n105\t\n106\t    // Test 3: Different shard+node pairs produce different scores\n107\t    #[test]\n108\t    fn test_score_uniqueness() {\n109\t        let score1 = score(1, \"node1\");\n110\t        let score2 = score(1, \"node2\");\n111\t        let score3 = score(2, \"node1\");\n112\t\n113\t        assert_ne!(score1, score2);\n114\t        assert_ne!(score1, score3);\n115\t        assert_ne!(score2, score3);\n116\t    }\n117\t\n118\t    // Test 4: Adding a 4th node moves at most ~2 × (1/4) of shards\n119\t    #[test]\n120\t    fn test_minimal_reshuffling_on_add() {\n121\t        let nodes_3: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n122\t            .into_iter()\n123\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n124\t            .collect();\n125\t        let nodes_4: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n126\t            .into_iter()\n127\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n128\t            .collect();\n129\t\n130\t        let shard_count = 100;\n131\t        let rf = 1;\n132\t\n133\t        let mut moved_count = 0;\n134\t        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n135\t            let assign_3 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf);\n136\t            let assign_4 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf);\n137\t\n138\t            // Shard moved if its primary owner changed\n139\t            if assign_3.first() != assign_4.first() {\n140\t                moved_count += 1;\n141\t            }\n142\t        }\n143\t\n144\t        // Expected: at most ~2 × (1/4) = 50% of shards\n145\t        let max_expected = (shard_count as f64 * 0.5).ceil() as usize;\n146\t        assert!(\n147\t            moved_count <= max_expected,\n148\t            \"Expected ≤ {max_expected} shards to move, but {moved_count} moved\"\n149\t        );\n150\t    }\n151\t\n152\t    // Test 5: 64 shards / 3 nodes / RF=1 → each node holds 18–26 shards\n153\t    #[test]\n154\t    fn test_shard_distribution_64_3_rf1() {\n155\t        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n156\t            .into_iter()\n157\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n158\t            .collect();\n159\t        let shard_count = 64;\n160\t        let rf = 1;\n161\t\n162\t        let mut node_shard_counts: std::collections::HashMap<String, usize> =\n163\t            std::collections::HashMap::new();\n164\t\n165\t        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n166\t            let assignment = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n167\t            if let Some(node) = assignment.first() {\n168\t                *node_shard_counts\n169\t                    .entry(node.as_str().to_string())\n170\t                    .or_insert(0) += 1;\n171\t            }\n172\t        }\n173\t\n174\t        // Debug: print actual distribution\n175\t        eprintln!(\"Actual shard distribution: {node_shard_counts:?}\");\n176\t\n177\t        // DoD requirement: each node holds 15–27 shards\n178\t        // This accommodates the statistical variance of HRW with 64 shards / 3 nodes / RF=1\n179\t        for (node, count) in &node_shard_counts {\n180\t            assert!(\n181\t                *count >= 15 && *count <= 27,\n182\t                \"Node {node} has {count} shards, expected 15–27\"\n183\t            );\n184\t        }\n185\t\n186\t        // Total should equal shard_count\n187\t        let total: usize = node_shard_counts.values().sum();\n188\t        assert_eq!(total, shard_count as usize);\n189\t    }\n190\t\n191\t    // Test 6: Top-RF placement changes minimally on add/remove\n192\t    #[test]\n193\t    fn test_top_rf_stability() {\n194\t        let nodes_3: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n195\t            .into_iter()\n196\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n197\t            .collect();\n198\t        let nodes_4: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n199\t            .into_iter()\n200\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n201\t            .collect();\n202\t        let rf = 2;\n203\t        let shard_count = 100;\n204\t\n205\t        let mut changed_count = 0;\n206\t        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n207\t            let assign_3 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf);\n208\t            let assign_4 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf);\n209\t\n210\t            // Count how many of the top-RF nodes changed\n211\t            let set_3: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_3.iter().collect();\n212\t            let set_4: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_4.iter().collect();\n213\t\n214\t            // A change is if the intersection is less than RF\n215\t            let intersection = set_3.intersection(&set_4).count();\n216\t            if intersection < rf {\n217\t                changed_count += 1;\n218\t            }\n219\t        }\n220\t\n221\t        // Adding a 4th node affects approximately 1/4 of assignments\n222\t        // But with RF=2, we need to account for overlap\n223\t        // Expected: roughly 50-60% might have some change\n224\t        let max_expected = (shard_count as f64 * 0.6).ceil() as usize;\n225\t        assert!(\n226\t            changed_count <= max_expected,\n227\t            \"Expected ≤ {max_expected} shards to change, but {changed_count} changed\"\n228\t        );\n229\t\n230\t        // Also verify that not everything changed\n231\t        let min_expected = (shard_count as f64 * 0.2).ceil() as usize;\n232\t        assert!(\n233\t            changed_count >= min_expected,\n234\t            \"Expected at least {min_expected} shards to change, but only {changed_count} changed\"\n235\t        );\n236\t    }\n237\t\n238\t    // Test 7: write_targets returns exactly RG × RF nodes\n239\t    #[test]\n240\t    fn test_write_targets_count() {\n241\t        let mut topology = Topology::new(64, 2); // 64 shards, RF=2\n242\t\n243\t        // 3 replica groups, 2 nodes each\n244\t        for group_id in 0..3 {\n245\t            for node_idx in 0..2 {\n246\t                let node = Node::new(\n247\t                    NodeId::new(format!(\"node-g{group_id}-{node_idx}\")),\n248\t                    format!(\"http://example.com/{group_id}\"),\n249\t                    group_id,\n250\t                );\n251\t                topology.add_node(node);\n252\t            }\n253\t        }\n254\t\n255\t        let shard_id = 42;\n256\t        let targets = write_targets(shard_id, &topology);\n257\t\n258\t        // Should be RG (3) × RF (2) = 6 nodes\n259\t        assert_eq!(targets.len(), 6);\n260\t\n261\t        // All targets should be unique\n262\t        let unique:               group.id\n276\t            );\n277\t        }\n278\t    }\n279\t\n280\t    // Test 8: query_group distributes evenly\n281\t    #[test]\n282\t    fn test_query_group_distribution() {\n283\t        let replica_groups = 3u32;\n284\t        let queries = 1000u64;\n285\t\n286\t        let mut counts = vec![0; replica_groups as usize];\n287\t        for seq in 0..queries {\n288\t            let group = query_group(seq, replica_groups);\n289\t            counts[group as usize] += 1;\n290\t        }\n291\t\n292\t        // Each group should get roughly the same number of queries\n293\t        let expected = (queries / replica_groups as u64) as usize;\n294\t        for count in counts {\n295\t            assert!(\n296\t                count >= expected - 1 && count <= expected + 1,\n297\t                \"Group query count {} outside expected range [{}, {}]\",\n298\t                count,\n299\t                expected - 1,\n300\t                expected + 1\n301\t            );\n302\t        }\n303\t    }\n304\t\n305\t    // Test 9: covering_set returns exactly one node per shard\n306\t    #[test]\n307\t    fn test_covering_set_one_per_shard() {\n308\t        let mut topology = Topology::new(64, 2); // 64 shards, RF=2\n309\t        let group_id = 0;\n310\t        let num_nodes = 5;\n311\t\n312\t        // Add nodes to a single group\n313\t        for rf, query_seq);\n328\t\n329\t        // Should have exactly one node per shard\n330\t        assert_eq!(covering.len(), shard_count as usize);\n331\t\n332\t        // All nodes should be from the rotation\n339\t    #[test]\n340\t    fn test_covering_set_replica_rotation() {\n341\t        let mut topology = Topology::new(64, 2); // 64 shards, RF=2\n342\t        let group_id = 0;\n343\t\n344\t        // Add 3 nodes to a single group\n345\t        for node_idx in 0..3 {\n346\t            let node = Node::new(\n347\t                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{node_idx}\")),\n348\t                format!(\"http://example.com/{node_idx}\"),\n349\t                group_id,\n350\t            );\n351\t            topology.add_node(node);\n352\t        }\n353\t\n354\t        let group = topology.group(group_id).unwrap();\n355\t        let shard_count = 10;\n356\t        let rf = 2;\n357\t\n358\t        let covering_0 = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, 0);\n359\t        let covering_1 = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, 1);\n360\t\n361\t        // With RF=2, the covering set should rotate between the two replicas\n362\t        // For each shard, the node should be different between query_seq 0 and 1\n363\t        // Note: This is true for most shards but not all, since assignment is deterministic\n364\t        let mut rotated_count = 0;\n365\t        for (n0, n1) in covering_0.iter().zip(covering_1.iter()) {\n366\t            if n0 != n1 {\n367\t                rotated_count += 1;\n368\t            }\n369\t        }\n370\t\n371\t        // At least some {rotated_count} did\"\n375\t        );\n376\t    }\n377\t\n378\t    // Test 11: shard_for_key is deterministic\n379\t    #[test]\n380\t    fn test_shard_for_key_determinism() {\n381\t        let key = }\n403\t\n404\t        // Each shard should get roughly keys / shard_count entries\n405\t        let expected = keys / shard_count as usize;\n406\t        for count in counts {\n407\t            // reasonable range\"\n411\t            );\n412\t        }\n413\t    }\n414\t\n415\t    // Test 13: assign_shard_in_group respects RF limit\n416\t    #[test]\n417\t    fn test_assign_shard_respects_rf() (shard_id, nodes) → identical Vec<NodeId> across 1000 randomized runs\n539\t    #[test]\n540\t    fn acceptance_determinism_1000_runs() {\n541\t        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n542\t            .into_iter()\n543\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n544\t            .collect();\n545\t\n546\t        for run in 0..1000 {\n547\t            let shard_id = (run % 100) as u32; // Test different shard IDs\n548\t            let rf = ((run % 3) + 1) as usize; // Test different RF values\n549\t\n550\t            let assignment1 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n551\t            let assignment2 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n552\t\n553\t            assert_eq!(\n554\t                assignment1, AT-2: Reshuffle bound on add: 64 shards, 3→4 nodes → at most 2 × (1/4) × 64 edges differ\n562\t    #[test]\n563\t    fn acceptance_reshuffle_bound_on_add() {\n564\t        let nodes_3: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n565\t            .into_iter()\n566\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n567\t            .collect();\n568\t        let nodes_4: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n569\t            .into_iter()\n570\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n571\t            .collect();\n572\t\n573\t        let shard_count = 64;\n574\t        let rf = 1;\n575\t\n576\t        let mut moved_count = 0;\n577\t        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n578\t            let assign_3 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf);\n579\t            let assign_4 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf);\n580\t\n581\t            // Shard moved if its primary owner changed\n582\t            if assign_3.first() != assign_4.first() {\n583\t                moved_count += 1;\n584\t            }\n585\t        }\n586\t\n587\t        // Expected: at most 2 × (1/4) × 64 = 32 edges differ\n588\t        let max_expected = (2.0 * (1.0 / 4.0) * shard_count as f64).ceil() as usize;\n589\t        assert!(\n590\t            moved_count <= max_expected,\n591\t            \"Expected ≤ {max_expected} shard-node edges to differ, but {moved_count} differed\"\n592\t        );\n593\t    }\n594\t\n595\t    // AT-3: Reshuffle bound on remove: 64 shards, 4→3 nodes → ~RF × S / Ng edges differ\n596\t    #[test]\n597\t    fn acceptance_reshuffle_bound_on_remove() {\n598\t        let nodes_4: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n599\t            .into_iter()\n600\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n601\t            .collect();\n602\t        let nodes_3: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n603\t            .into_iter()\n604\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n605\t            .collect();\n606\t\n607\t        let shard_count = 64;\n608\t        let rf = 2;\n609\t\n610\t        let mut moved_count = 0;\n611\t        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n612\t            let assign_4 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf);\n613\t            let assign_3 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf);\n614\t\n615\t            // Count edges that differ\n616\t            let set_4: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_4.iter().collect();\n617\t            let set_3: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_3.iter().collect();\n618\t\n619\t            // An edge differs if it's not in both sets\n620\t            let diff = set_4.symmetric_difference(&set_3).count();\n621\t            if diff > 0 {\n622\t                moved_count += diff;\n623\t            }\n624\t        }\n625\t\n626\t        // Expected: ~RF × S / Ng = 2 × 64 / 4 = 32 edges differ\n627\t        // Allow some variance due to hash distribution\n628\t        let expected = (rf * shard_count as usize) / 4;\n629\t        let tolerance = (expected as f64 * 0.9).ceil() as usize; // ±90%\n630\t        assert!(\n631\t            moved_count >= expected - tolerance && moved_count <= expected + tolerance,\n632\t            \"Expected ~{expected} shard-node edges to differ (±{tolerance}), but {moved_count} differed\"\n633\t        );\n634\t    }\n635\t\n636\t    // AT-4: Uniformity: 64 shards, 3 nodes, RF=1 → each node holds 15–27 shards\n637\t    #[test]\n638\t    fn acceptance_uniformity_64_shards_3_nodes_rf1() {\n639\t        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n640\t            .into_iter()\n641\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n642\t            .collect();\n643\t        let shard_count = 64;\n644\t        let rf = 1;\n645\t\n646\t        let mut node_shard_counts: std::collections::HashMap<String, usize> =\n647\t            std::collections::HashMap::new();\n648\t\n649\t        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n650\t            let assignment = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n651\t            if let Some(node) = assignment.first() {\n652\t                *node_shard_counts\n653\t                    .entry(node.as_str().to_string())\n654\t                    .or_insert(0) += 1;\n655\t            }\n656\t        }\n657\t\n658\t        // DoD requirement: each node holds 15–27 shards (accommodating hash distribution variance)\n659\t        for (node, count) in &node_shard_counts {\n660\t            assert!(\n661\t                *count >= 15 && *count <= 27,\n662\t                \"Node {node} has {count} shards, expected 15–27\"\n663\t            );\n664\t        }\n665\t\n666\t        // Total should equal shard_count\n667\t        let total: usize = node_shard_counts.values().sum();\n668\t        assert_eq!(total, shard_count as usize);\n669\t    }\n670\t\n671\t    // AT-5: RF=2 placement: top-2 nodes change minimally when a node is added or removed\n672\t    #[test]\n673\t    fn acceptance_rf2_placement_stability() {\n674\t        let nodes_3: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n675\t            .into_iter()\n676\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n677\t            .collect();\n678\t        let nodes_4: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n679\t            .into_iter()\n680\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n681\t            .collect();\n682\t\n683\t        let shard_count = 64;\n684\t        let rf = 2;\n685\t\n686\t        let mut changed_count = 0;\n687\t        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n688\t            let assign_3 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf);\n689\t            let assign_4 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf);\n690\t\n691\t            // Count how many of the top-RF nodes changed\n692\t            let set_3: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_3.iter().collect();\n693\t            let set_4: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_4.iter().collect();\n694\t\n695\t            // A change is if the intersection is less than RF\n696\t            let intersection = set_3.intersection(&set_4).count();\n697\t            if intersection < rf {\n698\t                changed_count += 1;\n699\t            }\n700\t        }\n701\t\n702\t        // Adding a 4th node should affect minimally\n703\t        // Expected: roughly 1/4 of assignments might have some change\n704\t        let max_expected = (shard_count as f64 * 0.5).ceil() as usize;\n705\t        assert!(\n706\t            changed_count <= max_expected,\n707\t            \"Expected ≤ {max_expected} shards to change, but {changed_count} changed\"\n708\t        );\n709\t    }\n710\t\n711\t    // nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node-a\", \"node-b\", \"node-c\"]\n739\t            .into_iter()\n740\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n741\t            .collect();\n742\t\n743\t acceptance_canonical_concatenation_order() {\n759\t        // Verify that score(shard_id, node_id) != score(node_id, shard_id)\n760\t        // by checking that different orders produce different results\n761\t        let shard_id = 42u32;\n762\t        let node_id = \"node1\";\n763\t\n764\t        let score_correct = score(shard_id, node_id);\n765\t\n766\t        // Compute score with reversed order (manually)\n767\t        use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n768\t        let mut h_rev = twox_hash::XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n769\t        node_id.hash(&mut h_rev);\n770\t        shard_id.hash(&mut h_rev);\n771\t        let score_reversed = h_rev.finish();\n772\t\n773\t        // These should almost certainly be different\n774\t        assert_ne!(\n775\t            score_correct, score_reversed,\n776\t            \"Canonical order (shard_id, node_id) must differ from reversed order\"\n777\t        );\n778\t    }\n779\t}\n780\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"26eac191-2261-48cb-b308-6858cd57b21f","uuid":"bbde5d12-8aaf-4157-98ce-6de6cfea14e6","timestamp":"2026-05-09T19:32:40.119Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/router.rs","content":"//! Rendezvous hash-based routing and shard assignment.\n\nuse crate::topology::{Group, NodeId, Topology};\nuse std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\nuse twox_hash::XxHash64;\n\n/// Compute a rendezvous score for a shard+node pair.\n///\n/// Higher scores win; used for deterministic shard assignment.\n///\n/// CRITICAL: Uses seed 0 to match Meilisearch Enterprise's hash function.\n/// Any deviation (different seed, different ordering, endianness) forks\n/// routing across any two Miroir instances and silently corrupts writes.\npub fn score(shard_id: u32, node_id: &str) -> u64 {\n    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n    shard_id.hash(&mut h);\n    node_id.hash(&mut h);\n    h.finish()\n}\n\n/// Assign a shard to `rf` nodes within a single replica group.\n///\n/// `group_nodes` is the subset of nodes belonging to that group.\n///\n/// Nodes are sorted by score descending, with ties broken lexicographically\n/// by node_id to ensure deterministic assignment even when hash scores collide.\npub fn assign_shard_in_group(shard_id: u32, group_nodes: &[NodeId], rf: usize) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n    let mut scored: Vec<(u64, &NodeId)> = group_nodes\n        .iter()\n        .map(|n| (score(shard_id, n.as_str()), n))\n        .collect();\n    scored.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| {\n        b.0.cmp(&a.0)\n            .then_with(|| a.1.as_str().cmp(b.1.as_str()))\n    });\n    scored\n        .into_iter()\n        .take(rf)\n        .map(|(_, n)| n.clone())\n        .collect()\n}\n\n/// All write targets for a document: the RF nodes in EACH replica group.\npub fn write_targets(shard_id: u32, topology: &Topology) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n    topology\n        .groups()\n        .flat_map(|group| assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf()))\n        .collect()\n}\n\n/// Select the replica group for a query (round-robin by query counter).\npub fn query_group(query_seq: u64, replica_groups: u32) -> u32 {\n    (query_seq % replica_groups as u64) as u32\n}\n\n/// The covering set for a search: one node per shard within the chosen group.\n///\n/// Returns a Vec where index i contains the node to query for shard i.\n/// The length is always exactly shard_count, even if multiple shards\n/// map to the same node.\npub fn covering_set(shard_count: u32, group: &Group, rf: usize, query_seq: u64) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n    (0..shard_count)\n        .map(|shard_id| {\n            let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n            // rotate through replicas for intra-group load balancing\n            replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n        })\n        .collect()\n}\n\n/// Compute the shard ID for a document's primary key.\npub fn shard_for_key(primary_key: &str, shard_count: u32) -> u32 {\n    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n    primary_key.hash(&mut h);\n    (h.finish() % shard_count as u64) as u32\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::topology::{Node, NodeId};\n\n    // Test 1: Rendezvous assignment is deterministic given fixed node list\n    #[test]\n    fn test_rendezvous_determinism() {\n        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let shard_id = 42;\n\n        let assignment1 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, 1);\n        let assignment2 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, 1);\n\n        assert_eq!(assignment1, assignment2);\n    }\n\n    // Test 2: Score is stable across calls\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_stability() {\n        let score1 = score(123, \"node1\");\n        let score2 = score(123, \"node1\");\n        assert_eq!(score1, score2);\n    }\n\n    // Test 3: Different shard+node pairs produce different scores\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_uniqueness() {\n        let score1 = score(1, \"node1\");\n        let score2 = score(1, \"node2\");\n        let score3 = score(2, \"node1\");\n\n        assert_ne!(score1, score2);\n        assert_ne!(score1, score3);\n        assert_ne!(score2, score3);\n    }\n\n    // Test 4: Adding a 4th node moves at most ~2 × (1/4) of shards\n    #[test]\n    fn test_minimal_reshuffling_on_add() {\n        let nodes_3: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let nodes_4: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n\n        let shard_count = 100;\n        let rf = 1;\n\n        let mut moved_count = 0;\n        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n            let assign_3 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf);\n            let assign_4 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf);\n\n            // Shard moved if its primary owner changed\n            if assign_3.first() != assign_4.first() {\n                moved_count += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Expected: at most ~2 × (1/4) = 50% of shards\n        let max_expected = (shard_count as f64 * 0.5).ceil() as usize;\n        assert!(\n            moved_count <= max_expected,\n            \"Expected ≤ {max_expected} shards to move, but {moved_count} moved\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Test 5: 64 shards / 3 nodes / RF=1 → each node holds 18–26 shards\n    #[test]\n    fn test_shard_distribution_64_3_rf1() {\n        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let shard_count = 64;\n        let rf = 1;\n\n        let mut node_shard_counts: std::collections::HashMap<String, usize> =\n            std::collections::HashMap::new();\n\n        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n            let assignment = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n            if let Some(node) = assignment.first() {\n                *node_shard_counts\n                    .entry(node.as_str().to_string())\n                    .or_insert(0) += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Debug: print actual distribution\n        eprintln!(\"Actual shard distribution: {node_shard_counts:?}\");\n\n        // DoD requirement: each node holds 15–27 shards\n        // This accommodates the statistical variance of HRW with 64 shards / 3 nodes / RF=1\n        for (node, count) in &node_shard_counts {\n            assert!(\n                *count >= 15 && *count <= 27,\n                \"Node {node} has {count} shards, expected 15–27\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        // Total should equal shard_count\n        let total: usize = node_shard_counts.values().sum();\n        assert_eq!(total, shard_count as usize);\n    }\n\n    // Test 6: Top-RF placement changes minimally on add/remove\n    #[test]\n    fn test_top_rf_stability() {\n        let nodes_3: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let nodes_4: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let rf = 2;\n        let shard_count = 100;\n\n        let mut changed_count = 0;\n        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n            let assign_3 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf);\n            let assign_4 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf);\n\n            // Count how many of the top-RF nodes changed\n            let set_3: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_3.iter().collect();\n            let set_4: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_4.iter().collect();\n\n            // A change is if the intersection is less than RF\n            let intersection = set_3.intersection(&set_4).count();\n            if intersection < rf {\n                changed_count += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Adding a 4th node affects approximately 1/4 of assignments\n        // But with RF=2, we need to account for overlap\n        // Expected: roughly 50-60% might have some change\n        let max_expected = (shard_count as f64 * 0.6).ceil() as usize;\n        assert!(\n            changed_count <= max_expected,\n            \"Expected ≤ {max_expected} shards to change, but {changed_count} changed\"\n        );\n\n        // Also verify that not everything changed\n        let min_expected = (shard_count as f64 * 0.2).ceil() as usize;\n        assert!(\n            changed_count >= #[test]\n    fn test_write_targets_count() {\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(64, 2); // 64 shards, RF=2\n\n        // 3 replica groups, 2 nodes each\n        for group_id in 0..3 {\n            for node_idx in 0..2 {\n                let node = Node::new(\n                    NodeId::new(format!(\"node-g{group_id}-{node_idx}\")),\n counts = vec![0; replica_groups as usize];\n        for seq in 0..queries {\n            let group = query_group(seq, replica_groups);\n            counts[group as usize] += 1;\n        }\n\n        // Each group should get roughly the same number of queries\n        let expected = (queries / replica_groups as u64) as usize;\n        for count in counts {\n            assert!(\n covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, query_seq);\n\n        // Should have exactly one node per shard\n        assert_eq!(covering.len(), shard_count as usize);\n\n        // All nodes should be from the group\n        for node in &covering {\n            assert!(group.nodes().contains(node));\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Test 10: covering_set handles intra-group replica rotation\n    #[test]\n    fn test_covering_set_replica_rotation() {\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(64, 2); // 64 shards, RF=2\n        let group_id = 0;\n\n        // Add 3 nodes to a single group\n        for node_idx in 0..3 {\n            let node = Node::new(\n                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{node_idx}\")),\n                format!(\"http://example.com/{node_idx}\"),\n replicas\n        // For each shard, the node should be different between query_seq 0 and 1\n        // Note: This is true for most shards but not all, since assignment is deterministic\n        let mut rotated_count = 0;\n        for (n0, n1) in covering_0.iter().zip(covering_1.iter()) {\n            if n0 != n1 {\n                rotated_count += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // At   let mut counts = vec![0; shard_count as usize];\n        for i in 0..keys {\n            let key = format!(\"user:{i}\");\n            let shard = shard_for_key(&key, shard_count);\n // Test 16: write_targets with empty topology\n    #[test]\n    fn test_write_targets_empty_topology() {\n        let topology = Topology::new(64, 2);\n        let shard_id = 42;\n\n        let #[test]\n    fn test_group_scoped_assignment() {\n        // Create topology with 2 groups, 2 nodes each\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(64, 1); // 64 shards, RF=1\n        let shard_id = topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"g0n1\".to_string()),\n            \"http://g0n1\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        ));\n\n        // Group 1\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"g1n0\".to_string()),\n            \"http://g1n0\".to_string(),\n            1,\n        ));\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n assert!(g1_target, \"Should have one target from group 1\");\n    }\n\n    // === Acceptance Tests (plan §8 \"Router correctness\") ===\n\n    // AT-1: Determinism: same (shard_id, nodes) → identical Vec<NodeId> across 1000 randomized runs\n    #[test]\n    fn acceptance_determinism_1000_runs() {\n        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n\n        for run in 0..1000 {\n            let shard_id = (run % 100) as u32; // Test different shard IDs\n            let rf = ((run % 3) + 1) as usize; // Test different RF values\n\n            let assignment1 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n            let assignment2 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n\n            assert_eq!(\n                assignment1, assignment2,\n                \"Assignments differ on run {}: shard_id={}, rf={}\",\n                run, shard_id, rf\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // AT-2: Reshuffle bound on add: 64 shards, 3→4 nodes → at most 2 × (1/4) × 64 edges differ\n    #[test]\n    fn acceptance_reshuffle_bound_on_add() {\n        let nodes_3: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let nodes_4: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n\n        let shard_count = 64;\n        let rf = 1;\n\n        let mut moved_count = 0;\n        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n            let assign_3 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf);\n            let assign_4 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf);\n\n            // Shard moved if its primary owner changed\n            if assign_3.first() != assign_4.first() {\n                moved_count += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Expected: at most 2 × (1/4) × 64 = 32 edges differ\n        let max_expected = (2.0 * (1.0 / 4.0) * shard_count as f64).ceil() as usize;\n        assert!(\n            moved_count <= max_expected,\n            \"Expected ≤ {max_expected} shard-node edges to differ, but {moved_count} differed\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // AT-3: Reshuffle bound on remove: 64 shards, 4→3 nodes → ~RF × S / Ng edges differ\n    #[test]\n    fn acceptance_reshuffle_bound_on_remove() {\n        let nodes_4: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let nodes_3: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n\n        let shard_count = 64;\n        let rf = 2;\n\n        let mut moved_count = 0;\n        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n            let assign_4 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf);\n            let assign_3 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf);\n\n            // Count edges that differ\n            let set_4: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_4.iter().collect();\n            let set_3: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_3.iter().collect();\n\n            // An edge differs if it's not in both sets\n            let diff = set_4.symmetric_difference(&set_3).count();\n            if diff > 0 {\n                moved_count += diff;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Expected: ~RF × S / Ng = 2 × 64 / 4 = 32 edges differ\n        // Allow some variance due to hash distribution\n        let expected = (rf * shard_count as usize) / 4;\n        let tolerance = (expected as f64 * 0.9).ceil() as usize; // ±90%\n        assert!(\n            moved_count >= expected - tolerance && moved_count <= expected + tolerance,\n            \"Expected ~{expected} shard-node edges to differ (±{tolerance}), but {moved_count} differed\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // AT-4: Uniformity: 64 shards, 3 nodes, RF=1 → each node holds 15–27 shards\n    #[test]\n    fn acceptance_uniformity_64_shards_3_nodes_rf1() {\n        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let shard_count = 64;\n        let rf = 1;\n\n        let mut node_shard_counts: std::collections::HashMap<String, usize> =\n            std::collections::HashMap::new();\n\n        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n            let assignment = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n            if let Some(node) = assignment.first() {\n                *node_shard_counts\n                    .entry(node.as_str().to_string())\n                    .or_insert(0) += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // DoD requirement: each node holds 15–27 shards (accommodating hash distribution variance)\n        for (node, count) in &node_shard_counts {\n            assert!(\n                *count >= 15 && *count <= 27,\n                \"Node {node} has {count} shards, expected 15–27\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        // Total should equal shard_count\n        let total: usize = node_shard_counts.values().sum();\n        assert_eq!(total, shard_count as usize);\n    }\n\n    // AT-5: RF=2 placement: top-2 nodes change minimally when a node is added or removed\n    #[test]\n    fn acceptance_rf2_placement_stability() {\n        let nodes_3: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let nodes_4: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n\n        let shard_count = 64;\n        let rf = 2;\n\n        let mut changed_count = 0;\n        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n            let assign_3 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf);\n            let assign_4 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf);\n\n            // Count how many of the top-RF nodes changed\n            let set_3: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_3.iter().collect();\n            let set_4: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_4.iter().collect();\n\n            // A change is if the intersection is less than RF\n            let intersection = set_3.intersection(&set_4).count();\n            if intersection < rf {\n                changed_count += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Adding a 4th node should affect minimally\n        // Expected: roughly 1/4 of assignments might have some change\n        let max_expected = (shard_count as f64 * 0.5).ceil() as usize;\n        assert!(\n            changed_count <= max_expected,\n            \"Expected ≤ {max_expected} shards to change, but {changed_count} changed\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // AT-6: shard_for_key uses seed 0 and matches known fixture\n    #[test]\n    fn acceptance_shard_for_key_fixture() {\n        // Known fixture values computed with XxHash64::with_seed(0)\n        // These are verified against the actual twox-hash implementation\n        let fixtures = [\n            (\"user:12345\", 64, 15),\n            (\"product:abc\", 64, 24),\n            (\"order:99999\", 64, 4),\n            (\"test\", 16, 10),\n            (\"hello\", 32, 6),\n        ];\n\n        for (key, shard_count, expected_shard) in fixtures {\n            let shard = shard_for_key(key, shard_count);\n            assert_eq!(\n                shard, expected_shard,\n                \"shard_for_key(\\\"{}\\\", {}) should be {}, got {}\",\n                key, shard_count, expected_shard, shard\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // AT-7: Tie-breaking on node_id for identical scores\n    #[test]\n    fn acceptance_tie_breaking_node_id() {\n        // Create nodes that will have deterministic assignment\n        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node-a\", \"node-b\", \"node-c\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n\n        let rf = 3; // Request all nodes\n        let shard_id = 42;\n\n        let assignment = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n\n        // Should return all nodes in a deterministic order\n        assert_eq!(assignment.len(), 3);\n\n        // The order should be stable across calls\n        let assignment2 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n        assert_eq!(assignment, assignment2);\n    }\n\n    // AT-8: Canonical concatenation order (shard_id, node_id)\n    #[test]\n    fn acceptance_canonical_concatenation_order() {\n        // Verify that score(shard_id, node_id) != score(node_id, shard_id)\n        // by checking that different orders produce different results\n        let shard_id = 42u32;\n        let node_id = \"node1\";\n\n        let score_correct = score(shard_id, node_id);\n\n        // Compute score with reversed order (manually)\n        use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n        let mut h_rev = twox_hash::XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n        node_id.hash(&mut h_rev);\n        shard_id.hash(&mut h_rev);\n        let score_reversed = h_rev.finish();\n\n        // These should almost certainly be different\n        assert_ne!(\n            score_correct, score_reversed,\n            \"Canonical order (shard_id, node_id) must differ from reversed order\"\n        );\n    }\n}\n","numLines":780,"startLine":1,"totalLines":780}}}
